etymology.description: 聲(声) (shēng, 'sound') + 殳(殳) (shū, 'hand holding a hammer or a mallet') = a stone chime being struck by a mallet
etymology.interpretation: The character 殸(qīng, 'sound of metal or stone') conveys the image of a stone chime being struck by a mallet, emphasizing the sound produced from this action. This representation highlights the character's focus on the auditory aspect of the interaction between stone and mallet.

parts.english: hand holding a hammer or a mallet
